Anteros Review: Is The Love Season Sniper Worth It?

Anteros brings a unique twist to the sniper role with his Mindless Heal ability. But in a world of skyrocketing stats, can this cherub still compete? We break down his kit, from his fast mana speed to the controversial translation errors that hyped him up.
Anteros Hero Review: When Cupid Shoots to Heal
Anteros, The Cupid of Love Returned: Fast Sniping With A Twist of Healing
Anteros is a 5-star Holy hero from the

event, originally released in February 2025. As a member of the Cupid family, he flutters onto the battlefield not just to deal damage, but to force your enemies into a confused state of benevolence. While he looks like an innocent cherub who might just stealing your chocolates, his kit is designed to shut down key enemy threats before they can ruin your day.

The artwork for Anteros leans heavily into the "steampunk cupid" aesthetic. He’s decked out with golden wings that look mechanically augmented and wields a heart-shaped hammer-arrow hybrid that looks like it could fix a clock or break a skull. It’s a refreshing, albeit slightly chaotic, take on the traditional Valentine’s theme. He looks like he’s having way too much fun about to smack someone with the power of love (and blunt force trauma).
When Anteros was released in early 2025, his stats were considered top-tier. However, fast forward to early 2026, and the landscape has shifted.
Still respectable, but modern hitters are pushing 1300+ base attack.
This is where he shows his age. With new tank monsters boasting 1300+ defense and 2250+ health, Anteros is becoming squishier by the month.
He is no longer a "stat monster," but he hasn't fallen into "obsolete" territory yet. He sits firmly in the
category for modern stat benchmarks—usable, but he needs emblems and limit breaks to survive against the latest meta defenses.
Special Skill: Love at First Sight
This skill is the bread and butter of Anteros. It’s a classic "Sniper + Control" package.
A crucial counter to the Dodge meta. If you see a rogue or a hero with a dodge buff, Anteros ignores it completely.
At Fast speed, this is a solid hit. It won't one-shot a full-health tank, but it will severely dent a flank or wing.

tops up your health. It’s a 2-for-1 swing.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Cupid family bonus is... niche. +20% healing for "Solo" heroes (heroes with no other family members on the team) is interesting but rarely a game-changer. It encourages rainbow teams or mixed-family compositions, but you likely won't build your team around this bonus.
Anteros is immune to Silence and Mindless Attack himself.
, or other mindless-attack inducing heroes, Anteros ignores their primary debilitation. He can charge up and shut
down. This makes him an excellent counter-pick in wars.
is simple and effective for a sniper. It helps close the gap between his older stats and modern defense values.
When Anteros dropped, he was seen as a solid, if not game-breaking, fast controller. He was the "safe" pick—reliable, fast, and annoying.
The speed of power creep has been merciless. His 1163 Power was "freaking awesome" in early 2025, but now, 4-star costumes are pushing 800+ and new 5-stars are starting at 1260+.
role. He is no longer a terrifying defense hero. His place is now on
, specifically to counter specific heroes (Dodgers, Mindless Attackers) or to lock down a problematic reviver/healer on the wing.
Anteros is a Cleric. You have two main schools of thought here, but given his fading survivability, one path stands out.
Keeps him alive long enough to fire his skill. His main utility is the
, not the damage. Dead heroes control no one.
Maximizes his 460% hit and Aether Power. Good if you use him strictly for clean-up teams.
With 1110 base defense, he risks getting one-shot by modern snipers before he can move.
path is recommended. You bring Anteros to shut down a threat, and he needs to survive the opening barrage to do that.
Fast Mindless Heal is one of the best shut-down mechanics in the game.
"Never Misses" makes him a hard counter to Rogues and Dodge buffers.
Passive immunity to Silence means he almost always gets to fire if he gets the tiles.
The Mindless Heal effect actually heals your team, providing secondary support.
In a meta dominated by "Hit All" and "Hit 3" monsters, single-target damage can feel slow.
His defensive stats are becoming a liability against the newest heavy hitters.
Being a Cleric makes him harder to speed up compared to other classes.
His best home. Use him to snipe the tank or control the reviver (Mother North/Alby) on the wing.
High tile damage and immunity to silence make him a very safe pick for Purple Titans, even if he isn't a dedicated defense downer.
While he is Fast, in Rush wars, his immunity to silence (often cast by opposing Rush tanks) is invaluable.
. Vivica drops defense, Jove softens them up, and Anteros finishes the key target while locking them down.
. A mix of overheal, dodge, and targeted silence to frustrate the enemy into submission.
Anteros is single target. If a Taunt hero fires, Anteros is forced to target them, potentially wasting his Mindless Heal on a hero who doesn't care (or who reflects it).
Be careful targeting enemy Monks or Clerics who might resist his Mindless Heal status ailment.

. He is an excellent carry hero. Focus on getting him to 4/80 immediately. He will teach you the value of mana control, which is the most important skill in high-level play. Do not worry about his stats "aging"—for a new roster, he is a god.

If you have him sitting at 3/70, think twice. Unless you desperately need a War depth hero for Purple tanks or a specific counter for Dodge/Silence heroes, your scopes might be better saved for a newer 1300+ power hero. He is worthy of emblems if you use him, but perhaps not an Alpha Aether unless you simply love his playstyle.
We cannot forget the "German Translation Bug" where players thought he hit three targets with Mindless Heal. As one forum user noted,
"I guess, the mindless heal to three... is just a wrong translation? Too bad, would have been nice."
It was indeed too good to be true.
Early reactions were mixed. While some called his stats "freaking awesome," others like the YouTuber McNaulty noted,
"Aside from the stats though, not the greatest hero."
Many players voiced frustration over his class.
"Just wish he was sorcerer class so I could charge in 7 tiles, too many clerics out there,"

Anteros is the definition of "Old Reliable." He isn't flashy, he doesn't destroy entire teams in one button press, and his stats aren't breaking any records in 2026. But when you absolutely, positively need to stop
from firing, Anteros is your cupid. He puts the "Special" in Special Skill blocking, and sometimes, love really does hurt.
A solid B+ hero who has aged gracefully into a utility role. Keep him for the wars, but maybe don't marry him for the stats.
A detailed review of Anteros, the 5-star Holy hero from the Season of Love. We analyze his Mindless Heal, stats, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
